posts.location: Renna's stall at the far end of the waygate marketplace crescent — dark-green oilcloth awning, early afternoon spring light, the boar tusks now on her bench and the commission formally recorded.
posts.narration:
Renna is bent over the bench recording the metal preference when Silas speaks. He does not speak to her. He speaks to Pell — or rather, he opens something toward Pell, and the words are only the surface of it.

"May I convey some things to you," he says, "to help you understand how to shape what you will make?"

He uses Order-Sense the way a man holds a door open — not to show the room, but to let the light through it. He finds the design Pell has in him: the morning on the ridge before the boar came, the cold working up through the mud into his knees, the way she stood three steps off his right shoulder without being asked because she had already read the ground and known where to be. He finds the boar — not the danger of it, but the weight of the moment just before it moved, when they were two people who had not yet said anything plainly and the world held them in that suspension. He finds the kill itself: not a proud thing, but a real one, taken cleanly and together, the first hard thing they did as a pair. He finds the walk back after, when the carcass was slung and neither of them spoke because neither of them needed to. He finds the tusks on the bench in front of them right…
